channel.title: Nature Materials
channel.description: Nature Materials is a multidisciplinary journal aimed at bringing together cutting-edge research across the entire spectrum of materials science and technology. Nature Materials covers all applied and fundamental aspects of the synthesis/processing, structure/composition, properties and performance of materials. Nature Materials provides a forum for the development of a common identity among materials scientists while encouraging researchers to cross established subdisciplinary lines. To achieve this, Nature Materials takes an interdisciplinary, integrated and balanced approach to all areas of materials research while fostering the exchange of ideas between scientists involved in different communities.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Connecting the dots for cooperative emission
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 31 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02560-3

Both superfluorescence and amplified spontaneous emission are observed in giant perovskite nanocrystals with the transition between these two cooperative emission regimes worked out by tuning temperature, excitation fluence and emitter density.
item.dc:title: Connecting the dots for cooperative emission
item.dc:creator: Yuan Liu
Kaifeng Wu

item.title: Transition between cooperative emission regimes in giant perovskite nanocrystals
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 31 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02544-3

The role of temperature and excitation density in promoting cooperative emission regimes such as superfluorescence and amplified spontaneous emission, as well as the transitions between such regimes in perovskite nanocrystals, is reported.
item.dc:title: Transition between cooperative emission regimes in giant perovskite nanocrystals
item.dc:creator: Etsuki Kobiyama
Gabriele Rainò
Yuliia Berezovska
Chenglian Zhu
Simon C. Boehme
Maryna I. Bodnarchuk
Rainer F. Mahrt
Maksym V. Kovalenko
Thilo Stöferle

item.title: Interlayer exciton flux amplification driven by strong exciton confinement
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 31 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02569-8

Nanoscale bandgap tuning in van der Waals heterobilayers using an electro-plasmonic nanocavity provides electrical control of exciton dynamics, producing localized interlayer exciton density gradients and enhanced diffusion currents in the nanoscale.
item.dc:title: Interlayer exciton flux amplification driven by strong exciton confinement
item.dc:creator: Hyeongwoo Lee
Taeyoung Moon
Artem N. Abramov
Ivan E. Kalantaevskii
Huitae Joo
Sujeong Kim
Vasily Kravtsov
Kyoung-Duck Park

item.title: The fourfold way to rupture in active solids
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 27 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02558-x

Quadrupolar topological defects act as organization centres for material failure in a biomimetic active nematic solid.
item.dc:title: The fourfold way to rupture in active solids
item.dc:creator: Daniel S. Seara
Vincenzo Vitelli

item.title: Mineral-originated bioelectronics for inhibition via lithium electrochemistry
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 27 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02526-5

A bioelectronic device incorporating triphylite (LiFePO4) as electrode is used for lithium-ion-specific inhibition of peripheral and central nervous system activities in rats and in mice.
item.dc:title: Mineral-originated bioelectronics for inhibition via lithium electrochemistry
item.dc:creator: Zhe Cheng
Tiantian Guo
Gangbin Yan
Jing Zhang
Jiping Yue
Chuanwang Yang
Suin Choi
Ananth Kamath
Saehyun Kim
Daniel S. Kohane
Chong Liu
Bozhi Tian

item.title: Narrowband helical emitter with frontier orbital confinement for stable deep-blue hybrid-tandem organic light-emitting diodes
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 27 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02529-2

Narrowband deep-blue organic light-emitting diodes combining high colour purity, efficiency and stability are realized by using multiresonance thermally activated delayed fluorescence emitters with a highly twisted helical structure.
item.dc:title: Narrowband helical emitter with frontier orbital confinement for stable deep-blue hybrid-tandem organic light-emitting diodes
item.dc:creator: Chuanqin Cheng
Minqiang Mai
Chenglong Li
Dongdong Zhang
Lian Duan
